below_hold_braces_but_over_constrains.braces: socket-z holds (no drag) — the below-hold resists the axial reaction in compression
below_hold_braces_but_over_constrains.over_constrains: every span shows a large zero-action wrist preload + socket tilt → peg cannot engage

design.below_hold: holder hand moved below+beside the socket to brace the axial reaction in compression

force_cut.finding: softening the press cuts the peak forces substantially (the 32 mm wedge was a brute-force ram)

honesty_statement: Numbers are the committed probe output; no value is hand-entered. The below-hold sweep PATCHES the holder geometry module constants to characterise an alternate configuration (not the shipped default); the matched-pair rollouts use the shipped env + controllers unchanged. The matched pair does NOT seat (≥0.9 precondition not met) — so the Gate-D / capability-gate / force-limit / oracle-calibration measurements are NOT run; this slice stops at the force-trust blocker, as pre-committed.

lateral_hold_drag.finding: even at the reduced force the clean-weld lateral bracket hold cannot brace the axial reaction — the FREE assembly drags down and the peg seats only part-way

purpose: After the founder S2 assembly-drag ruling (take the bracing path: cut the insertion force, then hold so the reference resists the reaction; active raise-to-meet mating is construct-invalid). Establish whether a competent BRACING cooperative hold can seat the peg within SAPIEN's drive-weld limits.
rationale: No SAPIEN drive-weld hold both braces the axial insertion reaction AND holds the socket orientation without over-constraint: the only cleanly-representable weld (the long lateral bracket, align ~1°) cannot brace (the assembly drags), while the hand-below-socket hold that DOES brace over-constrains the drive-weld at every anchor span (large zero-action wrist preload + socket tilt) so the peg cannot engage. This is a sim / force-trust blocker (the drive-weld cannot represent an axial-load-bearing cooperative grasp), NOT a Gate-D task finding (which is reserved for a clean, competent, FREE hold that still cannot seat). Per the Stage-2 §7 escalation it triggers the founder decision between the pre-committed S1b MuJoCo migrate path and an honest close; it is NOT resolved with active raise-to-meet mating (construct-invalid).
stage: S2 — bracing path + SAPIEN constraint-fidelity check
